competency_groups.title: Measurement and Quantitative Analysis
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to understanding matter, measurement uncertainties, laws of chemical combination, atomic theory, mole concept, stoichiometry, percentage composition, volumetric analysis, and quantitative estimation.
competencies: Understand the nature of matter and its properties including uncertainty in measurement
Apply Laws of Chemical Combination and Dalton's Atomic Theory to explain mass relationships
Calculate atomic/molecular masses using mole concept and molar masses
Perform stoichiometric calculations for percentage composition and reaction yields
Execute volumetric analysis techniques (titrations) with standard solutions like oxalic acid and sodium hydroxide
Determine concentration, strength of solutions, and perform quantitative estimations
competency_groups.unit_name: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry / Solutions
competency_groups.chapter_name: Unit 1: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry / Unit 1: Solutions

competency_groups.title: Atomic Structure and Quantum Mechanics
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering the discovery of sub-atomic particles, evolution of atomic models from Bohr to quantum mechanical model, electronic configurations, and periodic classification.
competencies: Trace the historical development of atomic models (Rutherford, Bohr) leading to the quantum mechanical model
Explain the behavior of electrons in hydrogen atom using Bohr's theory
Apply principles of Quantum Mechanics to describe electron distribution in atoms
Determine electronic configurations and classify elements into s-, p-, d-, f- blocks based on periodic table position
competency_groups.unit_name: Structure of Atom / Classification of Elements...
competency_groups.chapter_name: Unit 2: Structure of Atom / Unit 3: Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Chemical Bonding and Molecular Geometry
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving approaches to chemical bonding, types of bonds (ionic/covalent), bond parameters, VSEPR theory, hybridization, molecular orbital theory, and hydrogen bonding.
competencies: Differentiate between Kossel-Lewis approach and modern theories of chemical bonding
Predict the geometry of molecules using Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion (VSEPR) theory
Explain hybridization concepts and their role in molecular structure formation
Apply Molecular Orbital Theory to explain bonding in homonuclear diatomic molecules
competency_groups.chapter_name: Unit 4: Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics and Equilibrium Principles
competency_groups.description: Competencies focused on thermodynamic terms, enthalpy changes (calorimetry), spontaneity, Gibbs energy, equilibrium constants, Le Chatelier's principle, ionic equilibria, buffer solutions, and solubility.
competencies: Calculate internal energy change ($\Delta U$) and enthalpy change ($\Delta H$) using calorimetry
Determine the spontaneity of reactions based on Gibbs Energy changes and equilibrium conditions
Apply Le Chatelier's principle to predict shifts in chemical equilibria due to concentration/temperature changes
Analyze ionic equilibria including acid-base ionization, buffer solutions, and solubility products

competency_groups.title: Redox Reactions and Electrode Processes
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to classical ideas of oxidation/reduction, electron transfer concepts, oxidation numbers, redox reactions in electrode processes, electrochemical cells, batteries, and corrosion.
competencies: Identify oxidizing and reducing agents using oxidation number rules
competency_groups.chapter_name: Unit 7: Redox Reactions / Unit 2: Electrochemistry (Class XII)

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry Fundamentals and Mechanisms
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering tetravalence of carbon, structural representations, nomenclature, isomerism, reaction mechanisms, purification techniques (distillation, crystallization), qualitative/quantitative analysis, and functional group identification.
competencies: Represent organic compounds using various structural formulas and apply IUPAC nomenclature rules
Identify different types of isomers in organic molecules

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbons and Functional Group Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving classification of hydrocarbons (alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, aromatics), preparation methods, physical/chemical properties, reactions of haloalkanes/haloarenes, alcohols, phenols, ethers, carbonyl compounds, and amines.
competencies: Distinguish between saturated and unsaturated hydrocarbons based on structure and reactivity
Predict products of chemical reactions for various functional groups (halides, alcohols, ketones, etc.)
competency_groups.chapter_name: Unit 9: Hydrocarbons / Unit 6-10: Organic Chemistry Topics

competency_groups.title: d and f Block Elements and Coordination Compounds
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering position in periodic table, electronic configurations of transition elements, general properties (magnetic behavior, catalytic activity), lanthanoids/actinoids, Werner's theory, nomenclature, isomerism, bonding theories, and applications.
competencies: Explain the trends in physical and chemical properties across d-block and f-block series
competency_groups.unit_name: d and f Block Elements / Coordination Compounds
competency_groups.chapter_name: Unit 4: d and f Block Elements (Class XII) / Unit 5: Coordination Compounds

competency_groups.title: Surface Chemistry, Kinetics, and Biomolecules
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to adsorption/desorption phenomena, colloidal states (lyophilic/lyophobic), emulsions, reaction rates, factors affecting kinetics (temperature/concentration), collision theory, carbohydrates, proteins, enzymes, vitamins, nucleic acids, and hormones.
competencies: Differentiate between lyophilic and lyophobic sols and explain the role of emulsifying agents
competency_groups.chapter_name: Unit 10: Surface Chemistry (Class XII) / Unit 3: Kinetics / Unit 10: Biomolecules

competency_groups.title: Laboratory Skills and Practical Applications
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving basic laboratory techniques, characterization of substances (melting/boiling points), purification methods (crystallization), pH determination, volumetric analysis, qualitative inorganic analysis (cation/anion detection), chromatography, and project-based scientific investigations.
competencies: Perform standard laboratory operations including cutting glass tubes, boring corks, and setting up apparatus
